input.topic: Shadow Brokers Equation Group Tool Leak
input.notes: Historical corpus P0 incident for the 2016-2017 Shadow Brokers public release of exploitation tools assessed by researchers as linked to Equation Group, including the April 2017 Microsoft-focused release that exposed EternalBlue and related tooling. Draft as a bounded incident centered on the leak, public tool exposure, vendor triage, patching guidance, and downstream risk context later seen in WannaCry and NotPetya. Keep attribution conservative: distinguish researcher assessment of Equation Group linkage from unconfirmed claims about the leaker identity, acquisition path, or NSA operational custody. Do not duplicate the EternalBlue zero-day, WannaCry, or NotPetya articles; cross-link them as downstream context only.

preconditions: no open PR, issue, task, or live-main article already covers the Shadow Brokers Equation Group tool leak as a standalone incident
sources support a conservative distinction between public tool-release facts, vendor patch guidance, and assessed Equation Group linkage

history.note: Historical incident backlog seed from HIST-INC-017.
Requeued because PR #770 was a task-seed PR only; no article was created at the task output path.
